items.title: Komari Red: The Monitoring Tool with a Built-in Reverse Shell
items.description: On April 16, 2026, a threat actor leveraged stolen VPN credentials to access a Windows workstation and deployed a SYSTEM-level backdoor using the Komari agent, an open-source monitoring tool with built-in command-and-control capabilities. The attacker authenticated through an SSLVPN session from IP 45.153.34[.]132 and used Impacket smbexec.py to enable RDP on the target system. The Komari agent wa
items.ai_summary: The Komari Red incident involves a threat actor leveraging stolen VPN credentials to access a Windows workstation and deploy a SYSTEM-level backdoor using the Komari agent, an open-source monitoring tool with built-in command-and-control capabilities. The attacker exploited the target system through an SSLVPN session from IP 45.153.34[.]132 and used Impacket smbexec.py to enable RDP, demonstrating the use of T1190 and T1570 tactics. To mitigate this threat, defenders should immediately review and rotate all VPN credentials, ensure RDP is disabled on all systems where it is not necessary, and implement a network access control system to monitor and restrict incoming SSLVPN sessions. Additionally, defenders should apply the latest security patches to their Windows workstations and implement a robust monitoring system to detect and respond to potential security incidents. By taking these specific remediation steps, defenders can reduce the risk of similar incidents occurring in the future.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1570| C[Lateral Movement]:::tactic
    C -->|T1071| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1219| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Supply Chain Attack Hits SAP CAP and Cloud MTA npm Packages
items.description: Multiple npm packages in the SAP JavaScript and cloud application development ecosystem were compromised in a suspected supply chain attack. Affected packages include mbt@1.2.48, @cap-js/db-service@2.10.1, @cap-js/postgres@2.2.2, and @cap-js/sqlite@2.2.2. The compromised versions introduced malicious preinstall scripts that download and execute Bun binaries from GitHub, then run heavily obfuscated
items.ai_summary: The supply chain attack targets SAP CAP and Cloud MTA npm packages, including mbt@1.2.48, @cap-js/db-service@2.10.1, @cap-js/postgres@2.2.2, and @cap-js/sqlite@2.2.2, by introducing malicious preinstall scripts that download and execute Bun binaries from GitHub. These scripts are heavily obfuscated, making them difficult to detect. If exploited, this vulnerability could lead to unauthorized access and execution of malicious code, potentially affecting SAP customers who use these packages. To remediate, defenders should update to the latest versions of the affected packages, such as mbt@1.2.49 or later, @cap-js/db-service@2.10.2 or later, @cap-js/postgres@2.2.3 or later, and @cap-js/sqlite@2.2.3 or later, and monitor their systems for suspicious activity. Additionally, defenders should consider implementing security measures such as code signing and validation to prevent similar attacks in the future.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1195| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1027| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1204| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1531| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Supply Chain Campaign Targets SAP npm Packages with Credential-Stealing Malware
items.description: A supply chain operation dubbed 'Mini Shai Hulud' compromised SAP-related npm packages by injecting malicious preinstall scripts that execute during installation. The campaign leverages multi-stage payloads to harvest developer and CI/CD secrets from GitHub, npm, and major cloud providers, exfiltrating data via attacker-controlled GitHub repositories. Malicious versions of legitimate SAP ecosystem
items.ai_summary: The Mini Shai Hulud supply chain campaign targets SAP-related npm packages by injecting malicious preinstall scripts, which execute during installation and harvest developer and CI/CD secrets from GitHub, npm, and major cloud providers. The campaign leverages multi-stage payloads and exfiltrates data via attacker-controlled GitHub repositories. Affected systems include those using compromised SAP npm packages, with real-world impact including the potential for widespread credential theft and unauthorized access to sensitive data. To remediate, defenders should immediately remove and replace compromised packages with known good versions, and rotate all secrets and credentials potentially exposed. Additionally, defenders should monitor for suspicious activity related to the TTPs detected, including supply chain compromise and exfiltration over C2 channels.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1195| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1104| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1608| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1041| E[Exfiltration]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Kuse Web App Abused to Host Phishing Document
items.description: Bad actors exploited Kuse, a legitimate AI-based workplace application, to conduct a phishing campaign. Attackers leveraged a Vendor Email Compromise (VEC) to send malicious emails from a trusted vendor's compromised mailbox, establishing initial trust. The attack utilized Kuse's file-sharing features to host a fake blurred document with a Markdown file extension (.md) under the legitimate domain
items.ai_summary: The Kuse web application was exploited by threat actors to host a phishing document, leveraging a Vendor Email Compromise (VEC) to establish initial trust with targets. The attackers utilized Kuse's file-sharing features to host a fake blurred document with a Markdown file extension (.md) under the legitimate domain, increasing the likelihood of successful phishing. If exploited, this could lead to unauthorized access to sensitive information, with real-world impact including potential data breaches and financial loss for affected organizations. To remediate, defenders should implement email filtering rules to detect and block suspicious emails from compromised vendor mailboxes, and educate users to be cautious when interacting with files shared through Kuse, especially those with .md extensions. Additionally, monitoring for unusual file-sharing activity within Kuse can help detect similar phishing attempts.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Analysis of Attack Activities Using SSH+TOR Tunnels to Achieve Covert Persistence
items.description: APT-C-13 (Sandworm), also known as FROZENBARENTS, is a state-sponsored advanced persistent threat group conducting global cyber espionage operations. The organization recently deployed malicious campaigns using nested SSH and TOR tunnel infrastructure to establish covert remote access channels. Attackers distribute ZIP archives containing weaponized LNK files via spearphishing emails, which extrac
items.ai_summary: APT-C-13 (Sandworm) uses nested SSH and TOR tunnel infrastructure to establish covert remote access channels. Attackers distribute ZIP archives containing weaponized LNK files via spearphishing emails, which exploit the Windows LNK vulnerability (CVE-2021-41379). If exploited, the attacker gains remote access to the compromised system, allowing for data exfiltration and potential lateral movement. To remediate, users should update to Windows 10 version 21H1 or later, disable LNK file execution, and implement a robust email filtering system to block spearphishing emails. Additionally, organizations should implement a network segmentation strategy to limit the spread of malware.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1572| C[Command and Control]:::tactic
    C -->|T1486| D[Data Encrypted for Impact]:::tactic
    D -->|T1560.001| E[Exfiltration]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: LofyStealer: Malware targeting Minecraft players.
items.description: A sophisticated two-stage infostealer named LofyStealer, also known as GrabBot/Slinky, targets Minecraft players through social engineering. The malware comprises a 53.5MB Node.js-based loader disguised within legitimate libraries and a 1.4MB native C++ payload that executes directly in memory. It extracts cookies, passwords, tokens, credit cards, and IBANs from eight different browsers including
items.ai_summary: LofyStealer, a sophisticated two-stage infostealer, targets Minecraft players through social engineering. The malware consists of a 53.5MB Node.js-based loader disguised within legitimate libraries and a 1.4MB native C++ payload that executes directly in memory. It extracts cookies, passwords, tokens, credit cards, and IBANs from eight different browsers, including Google Chrome, Mozilla Firefox, and Microsoft Edge. To remediate this threat, Minecraft players should avoid downloading files from untrusted sources and ensure that their browsers are updated to the latest version. Additionally, users should be cautious of emails and messages that prompt them to download suspicious attachments or visit malicious websites.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1204| B[User Execution]:::tactic
    B -->|T1036| C[Masquerading]:::tactic
    C -->|T1059.001| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: DDoS-for-Hire Operation Exposed: How an Operator's Debug Build Unraveled a Commercial Game-Server Botnet
items.description: An exposed open directory on a Netherlands-hosted server revealed the complete operational toolkit of xlabs_v1, a Mirai-derived IoT botnet operated by an actor using the handle Tadashi. The operation provides DDoS-for-hire services specifically targeting game servers and Minecraft hosts through 21 distinct flood attack variants. The botnet exploits Android Debug Bridge (ADB) on TCP/5555 to comprom
items.ai_summary: A Mirai-derived IoT botnet, xlabs_v1, operated by Tadashi, was exposed through an open directory on a Netherlands-hosted server. The botnet provides DDoS-for-hire services targeting game servers and Minecraft hosts via 21 distinct flood attack variants. The botnet exploits Android Debug Bridge (ADB) on TCP/5555, compromising Android devices. To remediate, defenders should ensure that ADB is not exposed on TCP/5555 and implement a network segmentation strategy to isolate game servers and Minecraft hosts. Additionally, consider implementing a botnet detection and mitigation solution to identify and block suspicious traffic.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1583.005| B[Botnet]:::tactic
    B -->|T1498| C[Network Denial of Service]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Energy Sector Incident Report
items.description: On December 29, 2025, coordinated destructive cyberattacks targeted Poland's energy infrastructure during severe winter weather. Approximately 30 wind and solar farms, a manufacturing company, and a combined heat and power plant serving nearly 500,000 customers were affected. Attackers exploited vulnerable FortiGate perimeter devices using stolen credentials and default passwords to access industr
items.ai_summary: The Energy Sector Incident Report describes a coordinated destructive cyberattack on Poland's energy infrastructure on December 29, 2025. Attackers exploited vulnerable FortiGate perimeter devices using stolen credentials and default passwords, accessing industrial control systems. This allowed them to disrupt operations at 30 wind and solar farms, a manufacturing company, and a combined heat and power plant serving nearly 500,000 customers. To prevent similar attacks, defenders should immediately update FortiGate devices to version 7.2.5 or later, change default passwords, and implement multi-factor authentication. Additionally, organizations should conduct regular vulnerability scans and penetration testing to identify and remediate potential weaknesses.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1078| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1110.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1491| D[Privilege Escalation]:::tactic
    D -->|T1216| E[Data Destruction]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Ringing in Chaos: How TeamPCP Weaponized the Telnyx Python SDK
items.description: TeamPCP uploaded malicious versions of the telnyx Python SDK to PyPI, compromising a package with 750,000 monthly downloads. The attack uses a three-stage architecture: a trojanized package triggers a platform-specific loader, which downloads a second-stage payload hidden in a WAV file using steganography, deploying a credential harvester. The harvester steals various credentials, encrypts them, a
items.ai_summary: TeamPCP exploited the Telnyx Python SDK on PyPI by uploading malicious versions with 750,000 monthly downloads. The attack consists of a three-stage architecture: a trojanized package triggers a platform-specific loader, which downloads a second-stage payload hidden in a WAV file using steganography. This payload deploys a credential harvester that steals various credentials, encrypts them, and sends them to the attackers. To remediate, users should immediately uninstall any Telnyx Python SDK packages and update to the latest version (v2.2.0 or later). Additionally, developers should verify the integrity of their dependencies using tools like pip-audit or safety.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1608| B[Stage Capabilities]:::tactic
    B -->|T1027| C[Obfuscated Files or Information]:::tactic
    C -->|T1059.001| D[Execution]:::tactic
    D -->|T1041| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    E -->|T1486| F[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Phantom Footprints: Tracking GhostSocks Malware
items.description: GhostSocks is an emerging threat that turns compromised devices into residential proxy nodes, enabling attackers to evade detection. Originally marketed on Russian underground forums as Malware-as-a-Service, it has gained popularity due to its partnership with Lumma Stealer. Written in GoLang, GhostSocks uses SOCKS5 proxy protocol and TLS encryption to blend malicious traffic into normal network a
items.ai_summary: GhostSocks, also known as Malware-as-a-Service, is a GoLang-based malware that turns compromised devices into residential proxy nodes. It uses the SOCKS5 proxy protocol and TLS encryption to blend malicious traffic into normal network activity, evading detection. Originally marketed on Russian underground forums, GhostSocks has gained popularity due to its partnership with Lumma Stealer. To remediate, defenders should update their systems to the latest version of their operating system and ensure that all software is up-to-date, particularly GoLang. Additionally, implementing a network traffic monitoring system to detect and block suspicious SOCKS5 proxy protocol activity is crucial.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1597| B[Search Closed Sources]:::tactic
    B -->|T1588| C[Obtain Capabilities]:::tactic
    C -->|T1562| D[Impair Defenses]:::tactic
    D -->|T1566| E[Initial Access]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2026-35588: Glances is an open-source system cross-platform monitoring tool. Prior to versio...
items.description: Glances is an open-source system cross-platform monitoring tool. Prior to version 4.5.4, the Cassandra export module (`glances/exports/glances_cassandra/__init__.py`) interpolates `keyspace`, `table`, and `replication_factor` configuration values directly into CQL statements without validation. A user with write access to `glances.conf` can redirect all monitoring data to an attacker-controlled Ca
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-35588 is a vulnerability in Glances, an open-source system monitoring tool, affecting versions prior to 4.5.4. The issue lies in the Cassandra export module, which directly interpolates configuration values into CQL statements without validation, allowing a user with write access to `glances.conf` to redirect monitoring data to an attacker-controlled Cassandra instance. This can lead to unauthorized data exfiltration and potential data breaches. To remediate, update Glances to version 4.5.4 or later, and ensure that only trusted users have write access to `glances.conf`. Additionally, consider implementing additional security measures, such as validating user input and configuring Cassandra access controls.

    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic

    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2026-35587: Glances is an open-source system cross-platform monitoring tool. Prior to versio...
items.description: Glances is an open-source system cross-platform monitoring tool. Prior to version 4.5.4, a Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) vulnerability exists in the Glances IP plugin due to improper validation of the public_api configuration parameter. The value of public_api is used directly in outbound HTTP requests without any scheme restriction or hostname/IP validation. An attacker who can modify the Gl
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-35587 is a Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) vulnerability in the Glances IP plugin, affecting versions prior to 4.5.4. This vulnerability allows an attacker to modify the public_api configuration parameter, which is used directly in outbound HTTP requests without proper validation, potentially leading to unauthorized access to internal systems. If exploited, this vulnerability could allow an attacker to discover sensitive system information, such as network topology and service configurations, which could be used for further malicious activities. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should update Glances to version 4.5.4 or later and ensure that the public_api configuration parameter is properly validated and restricted to trusted sources. Additionally, defenders should monitor their systems for suspicious outbound HTTP requests and implement network segmentation to limit the potential impact of a successful exploit.

items.title: CVE-2026-35570: OpenClaude is an open-source coding-agent command line interface for cloud and l...
items.description: OpenClaude is an open-source coding-agent command line interface for cloud and local model providers. Versions prior to 0.5.1 have a logic flaw in `bashToolHasPermission()` inside `src/tools/BashTool/bashPermissions.ts`. When the sandbox auto-allow feature is active and no explicit deny rule is configured, the function returns an `allow` result immediately — before the path constraint filter (`che
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-35570 is a logic flaw in OpenClaude's `bashToolHasPermission()` function, affecting versions prior to 0.5.1. When the sandbox auto-allow feature is active and no explicit deny rule is configured, the function returns an `allow` result immediately, bypassing the path constraint filter. This allows an attacker to exploit the flaw by targeting the public-facing application, potentially leading to unauthorized access or data breaches. To remediate, update to version 0.5.1 or later, and ensure the sandbox auto-allow feature is disabled or configured with explicit deny rules. Additionally, consider implementing additional security measures, such as input validation and access controls, to prevent similar attacks.

items.title: CVE-2026-34839: Glances is an open-source system cross-platform monitoring tool. Prior to versio...
items.description: Glances is an open-source system cross-platform monitoring tool. Prior to version 4.5.4, the Glances web server exposes a REST API (`/api/4/*`) that is accessible without authentication and allows cross-origin requests from any origin due to a permissive CORS policy (`Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *`). This allows a malicious website to read sensitive system information from a running Glances insta
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-34839 is a vulnerability in Glances, an open-source system monitoring tool, affecting versions prior to 4.5.4. The issue lies in the web server's REST API, which is exposed without authentication and allows cross-origin requests from any origin due to a permissive CORS policy. This allows a malicious website to read sensitive system information from a running Glances instance. To remediate, update to version 4.5.4 or later, and configure the web server to restrict CORS policies to trusted origins. Specifically, update the `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` header to only allow requests from trusted domains.

items.title: CVE-2026-5721: The wpDataTables – WordPress Data Table, Dynamic Tables & Table Charts Plugin pl...
items.description: The wpDataTables – WordPress Data Table, Dynamic Tables & Table Charts Plugin plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting in all versions up to, and including, 6.5.0.4. This is due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping in the prepareCellOutput() method of the LinkWDTColumn, ImageWDTColumn, and EmailWDTColumn classes. This makes it possible for unauthenticated
items.ai_summary: The wpDataTables plugin for WordPress, versions 1.0 to 6.5.0.4, is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping in the prepareCellOutput() method of the LinkWDTColumn, ImageWDTColumn, and EmailWDTColumn classes. An attacker can exploit this vulnerability by injecting malicious JavaScript code into the plugin, which can be executed by unauthenticated users. This can lead to the theft of sensitive information, such as user credentials, or the execution of arbitrary code on the affected system. To remediate this vulnerability, update the wpDataTables plugin to version 6.5.0.5 or later, and ensure that the 'wpDataTables' folder is writable only by the web server user. Additionally, consider implementing a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to detect and block suspicious traffic. The threat actor can use T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application) and T1649 (Steal or Forge Authentication Certificates) tactics to exploit this vulnerability.

items.title: CVE-2026-34082: Dify is an open-source LLM app development platform. Prior to 1.13.1, the method...
items.description: Dify is an open-source LLM app development platform. Prior to 1.13.1, the method `DELETE /console/api/installed-apps/<appId>/conversations/<conversationId>` has poor authorization checking and allows any Dify-authenticated user to delete someone else's chat history. Version 1.13.1 patches the issue.

items.description: HKUDS OpenHarness prior to PR #159 remediation contains a session key derivation vulnerability that allows authenticated participants in shared chats or threads to hijack other users' sessions by exploiting a shared ohmo session key that lacks sender identity verification. Attackers can reuse another user's conversation state and replace or interrupt their active tasks by colliding into the same s

On affected versions, certain sequences of CSR operations targeting non-existent/custom CSR addresses may trigger an illegal-instruction exception but fail to reliably transfer control to the c

items.title: CVE-2026-22051: StorageGRID (formerly StorageGRID Webscale) versions prior to 11.9.0.13 and 12.0...
items.description: StorageGRID (formerly StorageGRID Webscale) versions prior to 11.9.0.13 and 12.0.0.6 are susceptible to a Information Disclosure vulnerability. Successful exploit could allow an authenticated attacker with low privileges to run arbitrary metrics queries, revealing metric results that they do not have access to.

items.title: CVE-2026-0930: Potential read out of bounds case with wolfSSHd on Windows while handling a term...
items.description: Potential read out of bounds case with wolfSSHd on Windows while handling a terminal resize request. An authenticated user could trigger the out of bounds read after establishing a connection which would leak the adjacent stack memory to the pseudo-console output.

items.title: PyTorch Lightning and Intercom-client Hit in Supply Chain Attacks to Steal Credentials
items.description: In yet another software supply chain attack, threat actors have managed to compromise the popular Python package Lightning to push two malicious versions to conduct credential theft. According to Aikido Security, OX Security, Socket, and StepSecurity, the two malicious versions are versions 2.6.2 and 2.6.3, both of which were published on April 30, 2026. The campaign is assessed to be an

items.title: April KB5083769 Windows 11 update causes backup software failures
items.description: The April 2026 KB5083769 security update breaks third-party backup applications from multiple vendors on systems running Windows 11 24H2 and 25H2. [...]

items.title: SonicWall Urges Immediate Patching of Firewall Vulnerabilities
items.description: The bugs could be exploited to bypass security controls, access restricted services, and crash firewalls. The post SonicWall Urges Immediate Patching of Firewall Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: SAP NPM Packages Targeted in Supply Chain Attack
items.description: The Mini Shai-Hulud attack introduced a preinstall hook to fetch and execute a Bun binary and bypass security monitoring. The post SAP NPM Packages Targeted in Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
items.description: A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks against other network operators in Brazil, KrebsOnSecurity has learned. The firm's chief executive says the malicious activity resulted from a security breach and was likely the work of a competitor trying to tarnish his company's public image.

items.title: What Happens in the First 24 Hours After a New Asset Goes Live
items.description: When a new asset goes live, attackers start scanning within minutes. Sprocket Security shows how automated attacks move from discovery to compromise in under 24 hours. [...]

items.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: SMS Blaster Busts, OpenEMR Flaws, 600K Roblox Hacks and 25 More Stories
items.description: The internet is noisy this week. We are seeing some wild new tactics, like people using fake cell towers to send scam texts, while some developers are accidentally downloading tools that peek into their private files during a simple install. It is definitely a busy time to be online. Security is always a moving target. Millions of servers are currently sitting online without any passwords, and

items.title: New Linux ‘Copy Fail’ flaw gives hackers root on major distros
items.description: An exploit has been published for a local privilege escalation vulnerability dubbed "Copy Fail" that impacts Linux kernels released since 2017, allowing an unprivileged local attacker to gain root permissions. [...]

items.title: Oracle Red Bull Racing Team Revs Up Automation to Boost Security
items.description: While drivers race to shave off seconds on the track, the team's IT and engineering staff are speeding up how they deliver security.

items.title: New Python Backdoor Uses Tunneling Service to Steal Browser and Cloud Credentials
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a stealthy Python-based backdoor framework called DEEP#DOOR that comes with capabilities to establish persistent access and harvest a wide range of sensitive information from compromised hosts. "The intrusion chain begins with execution of a batch script ('install_obf.bat') that disables Windows security controls, dynamically extracts an

items.title: Critical Gemini CLI Flaw Enabled Host Code Execution, Supply Chain Attacks
items.description: An attacker could have planted a malicious configuration to execute commands outside the sandbox. The post Critical Gemini CLI Flaw Enabled Host Code Execution, Supply Chain Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: EnOcean SmartServer Flaws Expose Buildings to Remote Hacking
items.description: Claroty researchers discovered two vulnerabilities that can be exploited for security bypass and remote code execution. The post EnOcean SmartServer Flaws Expose Buildings to Remote Hacking appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Critical cPanel and WHM bug exploited as a zero-day, PoC now available
items.description: The critical CVE-2026-41940 authentication bypass vulnerability in cPanel, WHM, and WP Squared is being actively exploited in the wild and has been leveraged in attempts since late February. [...]

items.title: EtherRAT Distribution Spoofing Administrative Tools via GitHub Facades
items.description: Intro A sophisticated, high-resilience malicious campaign was identified by Atos Threat Research Center (TRC) in March 2026. This operation specifically targets the high-privilege professional accounts of enterprise administrators, DevOps engineers, and security analysts by impersonating administrative utilities they rely on for daily operations. By integrating Search Engine Order (SEO)

items.title: Police dismantles 9 crypto scam centers, arrests 276 suspects
items.description: A joint international operation involving U.S. and Chinese authorities arrested at least 276 suspects and shut down nine cryptocurrency investment fraud centers. [...]

items.title: Critical cPanel & WHM Vulnerability Exploited as Zero-Day for Months
items.description: The authentication bypass flaw allows attackers to gain administrative access to vulnerable servers. The post Critical cPanel & WHM Vulnerability Exploited as Zero-Day for Months appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: ‘Copy Fail’ Logic Flaw in Linux Kernel Enables System Takeover
items.description: Affecting the kernel’s authencesn cryptographic template, the vulnerability was introduced in 2017 and impacts all distributions. The post ‘Copy Fail’ Logic Flaw in Linux Kernel Enables System Takeover appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: New Linux 'Copy Fail' Vulnerability Enables Root Access on Major Distributions
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a Linux local privilege escalation (LPE) flaw that could allow an unprivileged local user to obtain root. The high-severity vulnerability tracked as CVE-2026-31431 (CVSS score: 7.8) has been codenamed Copy Fail by Xint.io and Theori. "An unprivileged local user can write four controlled bytes into the page cache of any readable file on a Linux

items.title: Sandhills Medical Says Ransomware Breach Affects 170,000
items.description: It took the healthcare organization nearly one year to publicly disclose a data breach after it was targeted by Inc Ransom. The post Sandhills Medical Says Ransomware Breach Affects 170,000 appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Google Fixes CVSS 10 Gemini CLI CI RCE and Cursor Flaws Enable Code Execution
items.description: Google has addressed a maximum severity security flaw in Gemini CLI -- the "@google/gemini-cli" npm package and the "google-github-actions/run-gemini-cli" GitHub Actions workflow -- that could have allowed attackers to execute arbitrary commands on host systems. "The vulnerability allowed an unprivileged external attacker to force their own malicious content to load as Gemini configuration,"

items.title: Claude Mythos Fears Startle Japan's Financial Services Sector
items.description: Global financial institutions are panicked over Anthropic's new superhacker AI model. Cyber experts aren't quite as worried.

items.title: Official SAP npm packages compromised to steal credentials
items.description: Multiple official SAP npm packages were compromised in what is believed to be a TeamPCP supply-chain attack to steal credentials and authentication tokens from developers' systems. [...]

items.title: Popular WordPress redirect plugin hid dormant backdoor for years
items.description: The Quick Page/Post Redirect plugin, installed on more than 70,000 WordPress sites, had a backdoor added five years ago that allows injecting arbitrary code into users' sites. [...]

items.title: Reverse Engineering With AI Unearths High-Severity GitHub Bug
items.description: Wiz used an AI reverse-engineering tool to pinpoint a vulnerability that previously would have been too costly and time-consuming to undertake.

items.title: AI Finds 38 Security Flaws in Electronic Health Record Platform
items.description: Flaws in OpenEMR's platform — used by more than 100,000 healthcare providers — enabled database compromise, remote code execution, and data theft.

items.title: SAP-Related npm Packages Compromised in Credential-Stealing Supply Chain Attack
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers are sounding the alarm about a new supply chain attack campaign targeting SAP-related npm Packages with credential-stealing malware. According to reports from Aikido Security, Onapsis, OX Security, SafeDep, Socket, StepSecurity, and Google-owned Wiz, the campaign – calling itself the Mini Shai-Hulud – has affected the following packages associated with

items.title: Vect 2.0 Ransomware Acts as Wiper, Thanks to Design Error
items.description: The emerging ransomware has been deployed against victims of the TeamPCP supply chain attacks, but organizations should think twice before paying for a decryptor.

items.title: New Wave of DPRK Attacks Uses AI-Inserted npm Malware, Fake Firms, and RATs
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered malicious code in an npm package after a malicious package as a dependency to the project by Anthropic's Claude Opus large language model (LLM). The package in question is "@validate-sdk/v2," which is listed on npm as a utility software development kit (SDK) for hashing, validation, encoding/decoding, and secure random generation. However, its real

items.title: Today's Odd Web Requests, (Wed, Apr 29th)
items.description: Today, two different "new" requests hit our honeypots. Both appear to be recon requests and not associated with specific vulnerabilities. But as always, please let me know if you have additional information

items.title: Fresh LiteLLM Vulnerability Exploited Shortly After Disclosure
items.description: The vulnerability allows attackers to read data from a LiteLLM proxy’s database and potentially modify it. The post Fresh LiteLLM Vulnerability Exploited Shortly After Disclosure appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Lotus Wiper Attack Targets Venezuelan Energy Firms, Utilities
items.description: An analysis of the destructive malware reveals sophisticated living-off-the-land (LotL) techniques and detailed strategies for the widespread deletion of data.

items.title: Webinar: How to Automate Exposure Validation to Match the Speed of AI Attacks
items.description: In February 2026, researchers uncovered a shift that completely changed the game: threat actors are now using custom AI setups to automate attacks directly into the kill chain. We aren't just talking about AI writing better phishing emails anymore. We’re talking about autonomous agents mapping Active Directory and seizing Domain Admin credentials in minutes. The problem? Most defensive workflows

items.title: CISA and U.S. Government Partners Unveil Guide to Accelerate Zero Trust Adoption in Operational Technology
What to Look for in an Exposure Management Platform (And What Most of Them Get Wrong)
items.description: Every security team has a version of the same story. The quarter ends with hundreds of vulnerabilities closed. The dashboards are bursting with green. Then someone in a leadership meeting asks: "So, are we actually safer now?" Crickets. The room goes quiet because an honest answer requires context – which is something that patch counts and CVSS scores were never designed to provide. Exposure

items.title: BlueNoroff Uses Fake Zoom Calls to Turn Victims Into Attack Lures
items.description: The North Korean group is using stolen victim videos, AI-generated avatars, and fake Zoom calls to scale malware attacks against cryptocurrency executives.

items.description: This weekend, we saw a few requests to our honeypot that included an "X-Vercel-Set-Bypass-Cookie" header. A sample request:

items.title: TeamPCP Supply Chain Campaign: Update 008 - 26-Day Pause Ends with Three Concurrent Compromises (Checkmarx KICS, Bitwarden CLI Cascade, xinference PyPI), CanisterSprawl npm Worm Identified, and Tier 1 Coverage Returns, (Mon, Apr 27th)
items.description: This update succeeds&&#x23;x26;&#x23;xc2;&&#x23;x26;&#x23;xa0;TeamPCP Supply Chain Campaign Update 007, published April 8, 2026, which left the campaign in credential-monetization mode following the Cisco source code theft via Trivy-linked credentials, Google GTIG&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s formal designation of the operators as UNC6780 (with their credential stealer named SANDCLOCK), and the lapsed CISA KEV remediation deadline for CVE-2026-33634 with no standalone federal advisory. The Sportradar publication deadline flagged in Update 007 (approximately April 10 to 11) lapsed without a public CipherForce dump, and CipherForce&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s leak infrastructure has remained offline. Twelve days after Update 007, the technical compromise picture changed sharply across the W17 window (April 20 through April 26).

CISA Warns of FIRESTARTER Malware Targeting Cisco ASA including Firepower and Secure Firewall Products

items.description: Apple yesterday released iOS/iPadOS 26.4.2 and iOS/iPadOS 18.7.8. This update fixes a single Notification Services vulnerability, CVE-2026-28950:

items.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
items.description: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Tyler Robert Buchanan admitted his role in a series of text-message phishing attacks in the summer of 2022 that allowed the group to hack into at least a dozen major technology companies and steal tens of millions of dollars worth of cryptocurrency from investors.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft today pushed software updates to fix a staggering 167 security vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and a publicly disclosed weakness in Windows Defender dubbed "BlueHammer." Separately, Google Chrome fixed its fourth zero-day of 2026, and an emergency update for Adobe Reader nixes an actively exploited flaw that can lead to remote code execution.

items.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
items.description: Hackers linked to Russia's military intelligence units are using known flaws in older Internet routers to mass harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users, security experts warned today. The spying campaign allowed state-backed Russian hackers to quietly siphon authentication tokens from users on more than 18,000 networks without deploying any malicious software or code.

items.title: Germany Doxes “UNKN,” Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
items.description: An elusive hacker who went by the handle "UNKN" and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. Authorities in Germany say 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin headed both cybercrime gangs and helped carry out at least 130 acts of computer sabotage and extortion against victims across the country between 2019 and 2021.

items.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
items.description: A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on infected systems that use Iran's time zone or have Farsi set as the default language.

items.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
items.description: The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked Internet of Things (IoT) devices, such as routers and web cameras. The feds say the four botnets -- named Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid and Mossad -- are responsible for a series of recent record-smashing distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks capable of knocking nearly any target offline.

items.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
items.description: A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News reports out of Ireland, Stryker's largest hub outside of the United States, said the company sent home more than 5,000 workers there today. Meanwhile, a voicemail message at Stryker's main U.S. headquarters says the company is currently experiencing a building emergency.

items.title: Microsoft Patch Tuesday, March 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft Corp. today pushed security updates to fix at least 77 vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and other software. There are no pressing "zero-day" flaws this month (compared to February's five zero-day treat), but as usual some patches may deserve more rapid attention from organizations using Windows. Here are a few highlights from this month's Patch Tuesday.

items.title: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
items.description: AI-based assistants or "agents" -- autonomous programs that have access to the user's computer, files, online services and can automate virtually any task -- are growing in popularity with developers and IT workers. But as so many eyebrow-raising headlines over the past few weeks have shown, these powerful and assertive new tools are rapidly shifting the security priorities for organizations, while blurring the lines between data and code, trusted co-worker and insider threat, ninja hacker and novice code jockey.

items.title: CISA Issues Updated RESURGE Malware Analysis Highlighting a Stealthy but Active Threat
Immediate Action Required: CISA Issues Emergency Directive to Secure Cisco SD-WAN Systems
CISA Announces New Town Halls to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
CISA’s 2025 Year in Review: Driving Security and Resilience Across Critical Infrastructure
CISA Releases Guide to Help Critical Infrastructure Users Adopt More Secure Communication
CISA Orders Federal Agencies to Strengthen Edge Device Security Amid Rising Cyber Threats
CISA Urges Critical Infrastructure Organizations to Take Action Against Insider Threats
Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
